\subsection{Dense Primitive Synthesis Tables}

\noindent\footnotesize
These tables summarise the dense primitive-synthesis stage used before any repeated-launch memory is introduced.
The controller designs are fixed transition-aware LQR primitives executed through the real-flight-aligned actuator, command-lattice, and latency model.
R5 is therefore evidence about whether the primitive library contains physically executable manoeuvres and whether each primitive family contributes useful candidates for later survival screening.

\subsection{Survival Screening Tables}

\noindent\footnotesize
These tables report the fixed-controller survival replay used after dense synthesis.
The B-series evidence uses the current R7 survival stage; earlier R6 archived folders are historical diagnostics and are not the claim-bearing source for R8.
No LQR controller is retuned here: R7 only replays the frozen R5 candidates under broader environment and implementation variation, then passes eligible transition objects to compression.

\subsection{Library Compression and Outcome-Model Tables}

\noindent\footnotesize
These tables report how the R7 survival set is compressed into online primitive-library tiers.
Compression is coverage-aware: the medoid selection keeps primitive-family, transition-entry-class, and local speed-bin evidence instead of selecting only the highest nominal score.
The outcome model remains simulation-only evidence used by the outer-loop governor; it does not mutate the frozen primitive controllers.

\subsection{Governor Learning and Calibration Tables}

\noindent\footnotesize
These tables report the R10 full-domain governor-learning run.
R10 uses the frozen R8 library and learns only through the outer-loop governor: case-local repeated-launch flow belief is updated online, then aggregate R10 evidence freezes one bounded governor configuration for R11.
No primitive controller is retrained and no fan-layout-specific branch is introduced.

\subsection{Runtime and Reproducibility Tables}

\noindent\footnotesize
This table records the evidence-generation settings needed to interpret the appendix without opening result folders.
It separates the online controller contract from offline thesis logging: the primitive library and governor see only the canonical state and command interface, while post-processing tables are generated after the run.

\begin{longtblr}[
  caption = {Reproducibility and real-flight-aligned execution settings for the B/D-series evidence chain. Rows state what was fixed, what was varied, and why each setting is relevant for interpreting the appendix tables.},
  label = {tab:appendix-reproducibility-settings},
]{
  rows={font=\scriptsize},
  width=16.2cm,
  rowhead=1,
  rowsep=1pt,
  colsep=1pt,
  colspec = {
    X[3.10,l,m]
    X[5.00,l,m]
    X[8.10,l,m]
  },
}
\toprule

  \SetCell{m} \tiny\textbf{Setting}
  & \SetCell{m} \tiny\textbf{Value}
  & \SetCell{m} \tiny\textbf{Why it matters} \\
\midrule

  R5 dense primitive synthesis
  & B02; 102400 rollout rows; seed 1
  & Build the frozen transition-aware LQR primitive candidates under the executable actuator and latency model. \\

  R7 survival replay
  & B02; 1587 eligible transition objects
  & Replay the frozen R5 controllers under broader environment and implementation variation without retuning. \\

  R8 library tiers
  & Heavy, Balanced, Light, Super-light, No-cluster
  & Compress the survival set into online library tiers while preserving coverage over primitive family, entry class, and speed bin. \\

  R10 governor learning
  & D01; 1000 final launches; 10750 history launches
  & Use paired full-domain randomisation to freeze one bounded governor configuration for held-out validation. \\

  R11 held-out validation
  & D01; 8000 final launches; 86000 history launches
  & Validate the frozen R10 governor across the held-out L0--L7 environment-fidelity ladder. \\

  Repeated-launch histories
  & $h=0$, $h=3$, $h=10$, $h=30$
  & Compare no memory with online memory updated from the previous launches in the same physical case. \\

  R11 paired starts
  & 50 paired local cases per ladder (400 ladder cases total)
  & Reuse launch-state seeds across ladders, library tiers, and memory policies so speed-conditioned comparisons are fair. \\

  Primitive timing contract
  & 0.1 s horizon, 5 command slots, 0.02 s update
  & Keep every primitive compatible with the 0.1 s receding-horizon governor and 20 ms command update model. \\

  Command execution
  & 20\% lattice, $\{-1.0,-0.8,\ldots,0.8,1.0\}$
  & Execute the same quantised aggregate command contract in simulation that the hardware adapter will send to the Arduino path. \\

  Latency and actuator model
  & state delay 0.0229 s; command delay 0.073 s; actuator tau 0.050 s on all aggregate surfaces
  & Represent measured Vicon/command delay and actuator lag without embedding the Arduino/PPM chain in the online governor. \\

  Governor memory scope
  & case-local reset per final schedule row
  & Keep repeated-launch flow memory local to one physical case; no cross-case memory or final-run-only logic is used. \\

  Calibration strategy
  & deterministic bounded rule update; no black-box search
  & Avoid black-box optimiser behaviour while still allowing R10 aggregate evidence to set one frozen R11 governor. \\
\bottomrule
\end{longtblr}
